name: Garry Shandling
bio: Garry Shandling was an American comedian, actor, and writer known for his neurotic, self-deprecating stand-up and for pioneering meta-comedy on television. He created and starred in Its Garry Shandlings Show, which broke the fourth wall, and The Larry Sanders Show, an influential behind-the-scenes satire of late-night television. He was also a frequent guest host of The Tonight Show.

timeline.ev: First stand-up appearance on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson
Creates and stars in Its Garry Shandlings Show on Showtime
Begins recurring stint as guest host of The Tonight Show
Launches The Larry Sanders Show on HBO
The Larry Sanders Show ends after 89 episodes and 56 Emmy nominations

accolades: British Academy Television Award and a Primetime Emmy for The Larry Sanders Show writing (1998)
The Larry Sanders Show earned 56 Emmy nominations
American Comedy Award for Funniest Male Performer
